metadata.title: MailKit — Email on your Cloudflare domain in 30 minutes
metadata.description: Already on Cloudflare DNS? Automated Email Routing + Postmark SMTP setup with guided Gmail Send-As. $5 per mailbox.

hero.headlineLine1: Professional email on your Cloudflare domain
hero.headlineLine2: in 30 minutes — no tech skills needed.
hero.subhead: You start with a Cloudflare token and finish in your regular Gmail — both guided. We automate everything technical in between: routing, SMTP, DKIM. Built to reach the inbox, not spam.

mockup.alt: Gmail Compose window — the From dropdown shows your custom-domain addresses instead of @gmail.com

pricing.heading: Five dollars. One time. Done.
pricing.subhead: No subscription. No recurring fee. No surprise charges next month.
inclusions: Receiving and sending set up for you
SPF, DKIM, and DMARC records included
One address on your domain. Add more for $5 each
pricing.compare: Compare: Google Workspace $6/user/mo. ImprovMX $9/mo. Mailkit $5 once.
pricing.faqLink: Have questions before paying? Read the FAQ →

howItWorks.heading: Five steps. Guided all the way.
howItWorks.subheading: Nothing technical on your side — our wizard walks you through all of it.

stepToken.title: Create and paste your Cloudflare token
stepToken.body: Takes a few minutes in your Cloudflare dashboard. We show you exactly where to click.

step1.title: Name your mailbox
step1.body: Pick the address you want for your new mailbox. We create it on your domain.

step2.title: Automation does the setup
step2.body: We turn on email routing, provision SMTP, and add the DNS records.

step3.title: Waiting for confirmation
step3.body: Your DKIM records propagate and we verify them in the background. You can close the tab. We'll email you when it's done.

step4.title: Gmail Send-As
step4.body: We hand you four lines and walk you through pasting them into Gmail. Click the confirmation link it sends, and you're done.

problem.heading: Setting up email on your own domain takes about an hour — if nothing goes wrong.
problem.subhead: You can do it yourself for free — find a current guide, navigate three different dashboards, know which field gets what. That's where most people get stuck.

without.title: Manual setup
steps: Find an up-to-date guide on email routing
Create accounts and figure out multiple service dashboards
Copy and paste the right credentials into the right fields
Set up Gmail Send-As without mistakes
Hit a wall at any point and start over
Mail often ends up in spam, not the inbox

with.title: Automated
steps: Create and paste Cloudflare API token
Automation sets up receiving and sending for you
Your new branded mailbox is ready to send and receive

automation.title: Our setup fails
automation.body: If our automated setup fails on our end (Cloudflare or Postmark phase), you get a full refund within 24 hours. Automatic. No request needed.

functional.title: Your mailbox doesn't work
functional.body: If within 30 days you can't actually send email through your domain (even after our support), you get a full refund on request. Just email us.

items.q: Does this work with any domain?
items.a: Right now MailKit only works if your domain uses Cloudflare DNS. If your domain is on GoDaddy, Namecheap, Squarespace, or another registrar, you'll need to migrate nameservers to Cloudflare first. Cloudflare DNS is free and migration takes about 30 minutes plus DNS propagation up to 24 hours. After migration, MailKit setup runs as usual.

Your domain, DNS records, Cloudflare Email Routing and Gmail Send-As live on your own accounts, so receiving keeps working no matter what. Sending goes through MailKit's shared Postmark relay, and there is no separate fallback. MailKit is a small independent product — it can be discontinued at any time (for business, personal or infrastructure reasons), and we don't promise it will run for any set length of time. If it stops, sending pauses until you re-point Gmail at your own SMTP provider, and the one-time $5 is not refunded on shutdown. What always stays yours: your domain, DNS, routing and Gmail.
10 minutes of your active work, plus about 16 minutes of background DKIM verification. Total ~30 minutes from start to first sent email.

items.q: What if I have no technical skills?
items.a: That's exactly what we're here for. DNS, SMTP, DKIM, DMARC — that's our part. You paste four lines into Gmail following our step-by-step guide. No technical configuration required.
No. Email configuration doesn't touch your website settings. Email and web run on different DNS records, and we only modify the ones needed for email.

items.q: Do I have to keep paying every month?
items.a: No. MailKit is a one-time $5 fee per mailbox: no subscription, no recurring charges. That covers the full automated setup: Cloudflare Email Routing for receiving, Postmark SMTP for sending, and the guided Gmail Send-As walkthrough. Compared to Google Workspace at $6/user/month ($72 a year per mailbox), MailKit is a one-and-done payment.

items.q: Can I do this myself for free?
items.a: Yes, but expect an evening of documentation and wrong turns. You'd navigate Cloudflare Email Routing, Postmark SMTP configuration, five DNS records (MX, SPF, DKIM, DMARC, DKIM verification), and the Gmail Send-As setup, each requiring its own guide, its own dashboard, and a wait to see if the change took effect. MailKit automates the technical setup and guides you through the rest.

items.q: How is this different from Google Workspace?
items.a: Google Workspace gives you a separate inbox at $7/user/month forever: a new mailbox to check, a separate password, full migration if you stop paying. MailKit configures your existing Gmail to send and receive from your domain via Gmail Send-As. Same Gmail inbox, no migration, no monthly fee. The trade-off: with MailKit your domain email lives inside your personal Gmail, not in a Workspace tenant.

items.q: What happens if the setup fails halfway?
items.a: If our Cloudflare or Postmark automation fails, we issue an automatic full refund within 24 hours. The dashboard shows you which step failed and why. If you'd like to retry once we've fixed our side, we'll let you know. No extra charge if the failure was on our end.

items.q: Why doesn't Mailkit fully automate the Gmail step?
items.a: The Gmail API method that adds Send-As entries (gmail.settings.sendAs.create) is blocked on personal @gmail.com accounts. It requires Google Workspace with domain-wide delegation. We made that part a 3-minute guided walkthrough with copy-paste values instead. The other technical parts (Cloudflare + Postmark + DNS) are fully automated.

items.q: Is my Cloudflare API token safe with you?
items.a: While your setup is in progress we store the token encrypted at rest (AES-256) so you can resume from any device or browser without re-entering it. We delete it the moment the setup finishes, so it is never kept beyond your active setup, and it is never stored in plain text. The token can be scoped to the minimum permissions our wizard documents (Zone:DNS:Edit + Email Routing edits on the specific zone) and can be revoked from your Cloudflare dashboard at any time.

items.q: Why might my emails go to spam?
items.a: MailKit configures technically correct SPF policy, DKIM signature, and DMARC authentication. Whether email lands in a specific recipient's inbox depends on sender reputation, content, sending practices, and the recipient's policies — all outside MailKit's control. Domain warmup, recipient list hygiene, and consent compliance are the sender's job. Practical advice: send 10–20 emails per day in the first week from a fresh domain, then ramp up gradually.

items.q: How is this different from ImprovMX?
items.a: MailKit replaces the receiving piece (ImprovMX forwarding) with Cloudflare Email Routing and adds outbound sending via Postmark SMTP through Gmail Send-As. ImprovMX's free tier doesn't do outbound. You'll need to remove the existing MX records on your domain before setup. Our wizard detects conflicts and tells you exactly which records block the run.

items.q: Do you support Google Workspace mailboxes?
items.a: Not at launch. The Gmail Send-As walkthrough is built for personal @gmail.com accounts, and Workspace admins can already create domain mailboxes natively without MailKit. If there's enough demand, Workspace support is on the post-launch roadmap.

500 emails per day, 50 per hour, 5 per minute — per domain. These are standard limits on our shared email relay. For most small-business use, you'll never hit them. If you need higher throughput after your first month, email support@getmailkit.com. We review requests individually.

finalCta.heading: 30 minutes from now, you'll have email on your Cloudflare domain.
finalCta.subhead: Or your money back. No calls, no questions.

emptyState.title: Set up email on your domain

onboarding.getBody: A professional email address you send and receive from, right inside your existing Gmail.

dangerZone.title: Delete account
dangerZone.description: Deletes your account and setup history. Sign back in within 30 days to undo. After that, it's permanent.

deleteConfirm.title: Delete your account?
deleteConfirm.body: You'll be signed out and your account scheduled for deletion. Signing back in with the same Google account within 30 days restores everything. After 30 days your account and setup history are gone for good. Your DNS records and Gmail Send-As stay unchanged. Those live in Cloudflare and Gmail.

nsWarning.heading: {domain} doesn't appear to use Cloudflare DNS
nsWarning.body: MailKit currently requires Cloudflare DNS. Migrate your nameservers to Cloudflare first (free, about 30 minutes). After migration, run setup again.

setup.title: Connect your domain
setup.subtitle: Paste your Cloudflare API token to begin. About 10 minutes of your time, ~30 minutes total with DKIM verification.

step1.title: Step 1 — Paste your Cloudflare API token
step1.tokenHelp: Create at https://dash.cloudflare.com/profile/api-tokens. Template Custom token, scopes: Zone:Zone:Read, Zone:DNS:Edit, Zone:Zone Settings:Edit, Zone:Email Routing Rules:Edit (all four zone-scoped, with Zone Resource set to the specific zone) + Account:Email Routing Addresses:Edit (account-scoped).

step2.title: Step 2 — Name your mailbox
step2.mailboxHint: This becomes {local}@{domain}: lowercase letters, digits, dot, dash, underscore.
step2.mailboxRules: Lowercase letters, numbers, dot, dash, underscore.

awaitingVerify.title: Check your Gmail
awaitingVerify.body: Cloudflare sent a verification link to <mono>{email}</mono>. Click it, then come back to this tab.

done.title: You can now receive mail!
done.body: Emails to {mailbox}@{domain} now land in your Gmail.

failed.title: Setup didn't finish
failed.body: We ran into a problem finishing your setup.
failed.bodyCf: We ran into a problem setting up Cloudflare email routing.
failed.bodySmtp: We ran into a problem setting up sending for your domain.
failed.bodyGmail: We ran into a problem on the Gmail step.

errors.invalid_token: Token doesn't look right. Please try again.
errors.token_missing_permissions: Your token is missing required permissions. Add the ones listed in step 3 and try again.
errors.zone_not_found: Zone not found in your Cloudflare account.
errors.dns_rejected: We had trouble configuring DNS on your domain. This usually means your Cloudflare zone has a conflicting record or the API token doesn't have the right permissions. Restart from Step 1, or contact support@getmailkit.com if it keeps failing.
errors.rate_limited: Too many requests. Try again in a minute.
errors.cloudflare_unavailable: Cloudflare is temporarily unavailable. Try again in a few minutes.
errors.invalid_input: Something in the form looks off. Check it and try again.
errors.no_primary_email: Your Google account email is missing. Sign out and sign back in.
errors.run_not_found: We couldn't find your setup. Start a new one.
errors.run_wrong_state: Your setup got out of sync. Reopen this page to continue.
errors.run_corrupt: Your setup is missing some data. Start a new one.
errors.db_insert_failed: We couldn't save your setup. Try again.
errors.smtp_invalid_token: Our SMTP provider rejected the API key. Hold tight while we rotate it. Nothing for you to do.
errors.smtp_domain_taken: Our SMTP provider already knows this domain from a previous run. We picked up the existing setup.
errors.smtp_rate_limited: Our SMTP provider throttled our requests. Retrying shortly.
errors.smtp_unavailable: Our SMTP provider is temporarily unavailable. Try again in a few minutes.
errors.smtp_verify_timeout: Our SMTP provider could not verify your domain yet. DNS propagation can take a few minutes. Retry soon.
errors.smtp_state_unrecoverable: Our SMTP provider returned an incomplete setup for this domain. Please ping support.
errors.spf_conflict: Your SPF record ends in -all (strict policy). Soften it to ~all so our SMTP relay can send on your behalf, then retry.
errors.dns_write_failed: We could not write a DNS record cleanly. Details: {details}
errors.smtp_misconfigured: Our SMTP relay is not configured yet. This is on us, nothing to fix on your side. Ping support if you see this for more than a minute.
errors.postmark_invalid_token: Our SMTP provider rejected the API key. Hold tight while we rotate it. Nothing for you to do.
errors.postmark_domain_taken: Our SMTP provider already knows this domain from a previous run. We picked up the existing setup.
errors.postmark_rate_limited: Our SMTP provider throttled our requests. Retrying shortly.
errors.postmark_unavailable: Our SMTP provider is temporarily unavailable. Try again in a few minutes.
errors.postmark_dkim_timeout: DKIM verification timed out. DNS propagation can take a few minutes. Retry soon.

recheck.title: Waiting for DNS verification
recheck.body: DNS records are in place. Our SMTP provider hasn't confirmed them yet. Propagation usually takes a few minutes.

terminal.title: Now connect it to your Gmail to send from it.

gmailStep.title: Gmail Send-As — coming soon
gmailStep.body: Your Cloudflare routing and Postmark SMTP are ready. This last step connects your Gmail so you can send from {target}.

intro.title: Last step — Gmail Send-As
intro.subtitle: Add your new address to start sending from it.

senderInfo.title: Fill in sender info
senderInfo.body: Paste these into the "Add another email address" window. Leave "Treat as an alias" checked.

smtpSettings.title: SMTP settings
smtpSettings.body: Paste these four values, then click "Add Account".
smtpSettings.passwordWarning: Save this password. You'll need it if you set up Send-As again.

verificationEmail.title: Open the confirmation email
verificationEmail.body: Gmail emailed a confirmation link to {target}. It lands in this same inbox. Open the email, click the link, then press Confirm.

confirm.title: Confirm it works
confirm.body: Open Gmail → Compose → the From field should now let you pick {target}. Send a quick test email to yourself to be sure.
confirm.checkbox: I added the address and verified it in Gmail

done.title: You're all set!
done.body: You can now send and receive email right in Gmail from your new branded address, {target}.
done.warmupTip: Tip: warm up the domain gradually, 10–20 emails per day in the first week, then ramp up. Bursts from a fresh domain are the fastest way to land in spam.

errors.copyFailed: Your browser blocked clipboard access. Select the value and copy it by hand.
errors.checkboxRequired: Please confirm you've added and verified the address in Gmail.

step1.heading: Create a Cloudflare API Token
step1.scopeNote: Limit to one zone so the token is scoped to your domain only

step2.heading: Your domain and email address
step2.sub: We'll configure this exact address on Cloudflare Email Routing.

footer.tagline: Email on your domain. Setup in 30 minutes. Backed by a 30-day guarantee.
footer.copyright: © {year} Mailkit. Built by an independent founder.

error.title: Something broke on our end
error.body: An unexpected error happened. We've logged it and we're looking into it. You can try refreshing the page or heading back home.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.body: The page you're looking for doesn't exist or was moved. Russian-language pages were discontinued. Try the home page or check the FAQ.

cookieConsent.body: We use analytics cookies to understand how this site is used.
